rounded-none.description: Used when explicitly specifying that a design element shouldn't have rounded corners.

rounded-b.description: Used for data visualisation elements, tags, logos and toast components.

rounded-c.description: Used in cards, popovers, notifications, headers, navigation bars and side drawers.

rounded-e.description: Used in toggles, extended FABs and buttons.

rounded-f.description: Still to be agreed, added for future-proofing.

base.description: Used for strong attention to the foreground content and minimise background detail. It should always be used in conjunction with $container-base tokens.

neutral.description: Used for when some background detail still needs to be perceived without being a distraction. It should always be used in conjunction with $container-neutral tokens.

prominent.description: Use for visible and striking background blur effect. It should always be used in conjunction with $container-prominent tokens.

0px 2px 12px 0px rgba(0,0,0,0.12),
0px 2px 6px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.08),
inset 0px 0.5px 0px 0px rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05),
inset 0px 0px 0.5px 0px rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05)

inset 0px 0.5px 1px 0px rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08),
inset 0px 0px 0.5px 0px rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.12)
